author: cartographer
mission: TOOL-CONTEXT: Assess feasibility of context-adaptive tool descriptions in .opencode/tools/
disposition: FEASIBLE — requires coordinated changes across 3 layers, with a pragmatic Phase 0 workaround available immediately

1_where_used.summary: 30 static description strings in .opencode/tools/*.ts; built-in tools load from .txt files; consumed at session/tools.ts:89 → AI SDK tool() → LLM API

2_session_context_available.summary: NO sessionMode field exists in ToolContext. The 'mode' IS the agent name (agent field). No separate session mode concept exists in the tool execution layer.
2_session_context_available.mode_id_location: packages/opencode/src/acp-next/session.ts:22-31 — Info.modeId?: string (ephemeral, not in persisted session schema)
2_session_context_available.mode_id_derivation: modeId = agent.name (agents filtered by mode !== "subagent" && hidden !== true)
2_session_context_available.agent_field_proxy: For primary agents, `ToolContext.agent === sessionMode`. For subagents, agent is the current running subagent name.
2_session_context_available.session_persisted_fields: packages/opencode/src/session/session.ts — NO mode or sessionMode field in persisted schema

plugin_tool_signature.signature: tool({ description: string, args: Args, execute: (args, context) => Promise<ToolResult> })

internal_type_exists.status: DEFINED BUT UNUSED — marked '// TODO: remove this hack'
internal_type_exists.imports_zero: Not imported or referenced anywhere in production code

primary_workaround.name: tool.definition plugin hook
primary_workaround.signature: "tool.definition"?: (input: { toolID: string }, output: { description: string; parameters: any }) => Promise<void>
primary_workaround.limitation: Receives only { toolID } — NO session context, agent, or mode. Would need input extended to pass agent/session info.

secondary_precedent.name: describeTask()/describeSkill() append
secondary_precedent.mechanism: After tool.definition hook resolves, TaskTool and SkillTool descriptions are augmented by appending per-agent subagent/skill lists
secondary_precedent.note: This appends context-specific lists to static base descriptions — closest existing pattern to adaptive descriptions

tertiary_option.name: add description to context.extra and read it in execute()
tertiary_option.mechanism: Tool descriptions could be set as a field in context.extra, then tools could ignore their static description and log/use the dynamic one. But the LLM never sees this — it only sees the description passed at tool() registration time.

authority_modes.note: Controls tool/file permission gating, NOT description

6_tool_ranking.criteria: Benefit = (improvement in LLM tool selection accuracy from mode-tailored description) × (cost to change). Tools ranked by how much a mode-adaptive description would change agent behavior.

ranked.current_description_snippet: Run a bash command with structured logging and automatic smart-tool rerouting. Prefer smart_bun, smart_git, smart_grep, smart_find, smart_sd, read_source — use this only for commands without a smart equivalent.
ranked.why: In explore mode, bash should be discouraged (prefer smart tools). In deep/rescue mode, raw bash may be essential. The current 'prefer smart_*' list is static and doesn't reflect that explore agents have different tool availability.
ranked.mode_adaptation: Explore: 'Only use when no smart tool exists. Rerouting is automatic.'. Deep: 'Use for complex multi-step operations that smart tools cannot express.' Repair: 'Use cautiously — prefer targeted smart edits. Only for investigation, not mutation.'

ranked.current_description_snippet: Delegate work to agents or send coordination messages. Enforces spawn permissions, tracks lane lifecycle, generates structured task prompts, and logs everything to the coordination ledger.
ranked.why: In plan mode, delegation is the primary workflow. In explore mode, delegation is rare. In repair mode, delegation should target specific subagent types.
ranked.mode_adaptation: Plan: 'Delegate subtasks to subagents for parallel execution. Use for all lane workflow steps.'. Explore: 'Delegate only for independent research sub-tasks.'. Repair: 'Delegate only to specialized repair subagents (surgeon, scalpel).'

ranked.current_description_snippet: Plan lifecycle — propose new plans or revise existing ones.
ranked.why: In explore mode, planning is secondary to discovery. In plan mode, it's the primary tool. In repair mode, plans should be minimal revisions.
ranked.mode_adaptation: Plan: 'Propose detailed architecture plans with claim atoms, boundaries, consumer purpose.'. Explore: 'Skip planning — focus on discovery.'. Repair: 'Propose minimal, targeted repair plans only.'

ranked.current_description_snippet: Coordinate with the fleet — send messages or announce delegations.
ranked.why: In deep/multi-lane modes, coordination is critical. In explore mode, it's noise. In repair mode, coordination should be narrowly scoped.
ranked.mode_adaptation: Deep: 'Coordinate across all active lanes — use send for cross-lane messages, delegate for subtasks.'. Explore: 'Skip — coordination is for multi-lane workflows.'. Repair: 'Coordinate only blocker handoffs to coordinating agents.'

ranked.current_description_snippet: Read a source file and return a structured digest — imports, exports, key symbols, and optional focus on a specific symbol.
ranked.why: In explore mode, broad reads with summaries are preferred. In deep mode, focused reads into specific symbols. In repair mode, reads should target the failure boundary.
ranked.mode_adaptation: Explore: 'Use summary_only=true for broad scans. Focus on imports and exports.'. Deep: 'Use focus parameter for deep dives into specific symbols.'. Repair: 'Target the specific file and symbol identified in the error trace.'

ranked.current_description_snippet: Fragment producer — declare a file region this lane intends to modify.
ranked.why: Only relevant in multi-lane (parallel) work. In explore mode, this tool should be hidden or warned against. In repair mode, it's essential for shared-file coordination.
ranked.mode_adaptation: Deep/Plan: 'Declare regions for shared-file coordination between parallel lanes.'. Explore: 'Skip — fragments are for mutation, not exploration.'

ranked.current_description_snippet: Publication gates — publish findings/checkpoints or record prepublication verdicts.
ranked.why: Only relevant in plan/execute/review modes. In explore mode, this is dead weight that pollutes the tool list.
ranked.mode_adaptation: Plan: 'Use publish-finding to record out-of-scope discoveries.'. Explore: 'Do not use — publication is for post-discovery phases.'

6_tool_ranking.reason: These tools have narrow, mode-independent semantics. Adaptive descriptions would not meaningfully change LLM selection behavior.

7_dirty_files.summary: NO dirty files in the tool or plugin layer. All dirty files are session artifacts (analytics logs, heartbeat.jsonl, edit logs) and unrelated lane work (campaign/), adr/ directories.
unrelated_dirty: Session analytics files (20+ untracked/unstaged JSONL files)

8_sdk_vs_opencode_scope.verdict: MULTI-LAYER — requires changes in 3 packages, but a Phase 0 workaround is achievable purely in .opencode/tools/

packages/plugin.changes_needed: Extend tool() to accept description: string | ((ctx: ToolContext) => string) or add a description? param alongside the required string. Extend ToolContext to include mode?: string or sessionContext?: {...}.
packages/plugin.risk: MEDIUM — breaking change for all plugin tool consumers (30 .opencode/tools/ files + external plugin authors). Need version bump.
packages/plugin.alt_approach: Add a new optional `dynamicDescription?` field to tool() while keeping `description` as the required static fallback. This is non-breaking.

packages/opencode/src/tool.changes_needed: Wire DynamicDescription into Def. Thread mode/agent info through the description resolution in registry.tools(). Update session/tools.ts to resolve dynamic descriptions at build time.
packages/opencode/src/tool.risk: LOW-MEDIUM — DynamicDescription type already exists. The resolution point (registry.tools()) already has access to Agent.Info via describeTask/describeSkill pattern.

.opencode/tools.changes_needed: Adopt dynamic description syntax once SDK supports it. For Phase 0: use a pattern like `description: buildDescription(mode)` where buildDescription is a local helper that reads the mode from context or a convention-based lookup.
.opencode/tools.risk: LOW — these are user-owned files, not published packages.

phase_0_workaround.description: Change NO SDK code. Implement context-adaptive descriptions purely in .opencode/tools/ and the registry.tools() describeTask pattern.
phase_0_workaround.approach: 1. Extend the describeTask/describeSkill pattern to a generic describeTool(toolId, agent) in registry.ts. 2. Add a per-mode description table (object mapping agentName → description variant) as a convention in .opencode/tools/. 3. Registry.tools() reads the mode from agent.name and selects the variant. 4. No breaking changes, no SDK release needed.
phase_0_workaround.effort: ~200 lines across registry.ts + convention doc, + ~30 lines per tool file

smoking_guns.what: DynamicDescription type exists but is dead code, marked 'TODO: remove this hack'
smoking_guns.impact: The internal tool system already has the concept. The work was started then abandoned. Reviving it requires wiring it into Def and the resolution pipeline.

smoking_guns.what: tool.definition hook receives only { toolID } — no agent/session context
smoking_guns.impact: The plugin SDK's only description mutation hook is context-blind. Without extending its input signature, plugins cannot make context-aware decisions.

smoking_guns.what: describeTask()/describeSkill() already append per-agent text — the pattern exists
smoking_guns.impact: This is proof that context-dependent description augmentation is already happening for 2 tools. Generalizing this pattern to all tools is low-risk.

smoking_guns.what: No session mode in persisted session schema
smoking_guns.impact: Mode is NOT persisted. It's an in-memory concern in the ACP session manager. During prompt/tool resolution, mode is available as agent.name but not as a dedicated sessionMode field. Relying on agent.name as proxy is safe for primary agents but may break for subagents.

smoking_guns.what: Plugin ToolContext and built-in Tool.Context are diverged types
smoking_guns.impact: Any change to ToolContext (e.g., adding mode) must be mirrored in both types and the bridge at registry.ts:351. This is a recurring maintenance burden.

pre_existing_findings_relevant.finding: Two tools in registry are placeholders (delegate.ts, session-diff.ts) — erodes trust in tool registry
pre_existing_findings_relevant.relevance: high — context-adaptive descriptions should not be applied to placeholder tools until they're graduated

pre_existing_findings_relevant.finding: Bidirectional dependency: tool/registry.ts ↔ session/{todo,instruction} — fragile init order
pre_existing_findings_relevant.relevance: medium — adding mode-aware description resolution to registry.ts may exacerbate init-order fragility

phase_0_immediate.name: Generalize describeTask pattern to all tools
phase_0_immediate.approach: Add a describeTool(id, agent) helper that tools can optionally export a modeVariants map. Registry.tools() reads mode from agent.name and selects the variant. No SDK changes, no breaking changes.
phase_0_immediate.consumer_pattern: .opencode/tools/ files export a `modeDescriptions?: Record<string, string>` alongside their default tool.

phase_1_medium_term.name: Extend plugin SDK tool.definition hook with context
phase_1_medium_term.approach: Change tool.definition input from { toolID } to { toolID, agent, sessionID }. This is a non-breaking extension (additive). Then .opencode/tools/ plugins can use the hook to switch descriptions.

phase_2_long_term.name: Wire DynamicDescription into Def and plugin tool()
phase_2_long_term.approach: Make Def.description accept string | DynamicDescription. Make plugin tool() accept description: string | ((ctx) => string). Resolve at tool definition time (before prompt assembly). Remove the TODO hack.
phase_2_long_term.risk: MEDIUM — type-level breaking change, needs careful migration path
